johns-baked-pork-chops-stuffed-with-smok.name: John's Baked Pork Chops Stuffed With Smoked Gouda And Bacon
instructions: Combine Gouda cheese, bacon, parsley, and 1/8 teaspoon black pepper together in a bowl.

Cut each pork chop from one side through the middle horizontally to within 1/2 inch of the other side. Open the two sides and spread them out like an open book. Stuff cheese mixture into each pocket and close; secure with a wooden toothpick. Coat each stuffed pork chop with olive oil.

Whisk egg in a bowl. Mix bread crumbs, salt, and black pepper to taste together in a separate bowl. Carefully dip each stuffed pork chop in the egg; press chop into bread crumb mixture until evenly coated. Place breaded pork chops in a 9x13-inch casserole dish.

Bake in preheated oven until no longer pink in the center, about 45 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 63°C (63 degrees C). Garnish pork chops with cored apple, green onion, and red grapes.

johnsonville-brat-hot-tub.name: Johnsonville Brat Hot Tub
instructions: Preheat an outdoor grill for medium-low heat and lightly oil the grate.

Cook brats, covered, on preheated grill, turning often, until browned and an instant-read thermometer inserted into centers reads 71°C (70 degrees C). Transfer brats to foil baking pan. Increase grill heat to medium.
Place foil pan on grill; add beer, onions, and butter. Cover grill; simmer brats. Serve brats and onions on buns.

jrs-irish-whiskey-cocktail.name: Jr's Irish Whiskey Cocktail
instructions: Muddle lemon wedge and orange slice in a cocktail shaker.
Add Irish whiskey, amaretto, triple sec, bitters, and 1 cup of ice.
Fill a rocks glass with remaining 1/2 cup of ice and strain cocktail on top.
